reviews.review_title: The Ace Handset support needs adjustment
reviews.review_description: Yey, this game is cool. But It needs a lot of fine tuning to be matched with the original trigger reset of the ACE handset. This is the game that doesn't need monthly subscription, please fix it ASAP 🙏

developer_response.body: What value did you end up setting for your ace handset. the default we have of 14 is what our Ace Handset broke at. The value before the break should show in the gui where you configure the profile? I don't know if the newer handsets have a different break point. If you can stop by our discord we'd be glad to figure out what the issue was. https://discord.com/invite/GgQy6qugZU

reviews.review_description: you spawn in with a rifle default in your left hand and a pistol in your right. no easy way to table either. no real tutorial and setting up the basic setup is a nightmare. Whoever's trying to sell this game and saying that it's worth a 4.3 is out of their mind it is not worth it at all. it feels unfinished clunky and the user interface is terrible. don't waste your money on this app.

Great idea, hard to get going in. Start with no idea on how to use weapons, but give me one in each hand. Uses buttons to eject  magazine and rack the slide, with a “bump the bottom” to insert new magazine. This does not feel realistic at all. After trying to hit targets, do some odd drills, and then trying to debug why I hear zombies but cant see them through my AR walls, figured out how to turn off the walls and was rewarded with some lagging performance zombie (quest 2) attacks. Then went to play Arizona sunshine.
Zombies come randomly out of nowhere.  Guns are not really usable and the game all round feels like it needs alot of work to even be a playable game.

developer_response.body:
Did you run the room setup in headset when it popuped up? The game will not work without a valid room setup, as it's a mixed reality game. We haven't hand any reports of the zombies behaving poorly, or weapons not working well. can you stop by our discord so we can figure out what's going on?
https://discord.gg/ydzfCFAH

reviews.review_title: This thing is addictive!! But it rocks:)
reviews.review_description: You know what... I'm a vet with more than 20 years of combat rotations experience, most in SF units. I've been to places and seen people and stuff. I've fired tons of ammo during training and operations. I quit all these because I got bored at some point. Well... I've NEVER been so excited about my training... Even being a fresh 2nd lieutenant. Perhaps I feel so because this thing turned out to be a bridge to the great past, but whatever. I've spent two hours non-stop, sweating like $hit, hitting and missing, getting pissed about my performance in terms of time and so on. And I stopped my session due to the low battery warning. I don't have any word to describe this except for "masterpiece". Some minor issues exist, but even if there was 10 or 100 times more than I've really discovered, they mean nothing. The simulation is glorious! It's either a game or a training aid. It's for you to decide.

Be warned this game is addictive! The goal is to defend your home from a zombie assault. The awesome part is ITS YOUR HOME! Your floor plan is copied for the game. You can blast zombies through windows and doors. Really using home court advantage against the undead horde. While this sounds like every other game (and movie and tv show) it isn’t. The shooting feels real. Accuracy matters, shooting skills will translate but also improve. The game has a mode where you can run drills that I read about online. Drills used by shooting instructors to improve your gun handling and shooting. The developer is also selling a handset that your vr controller fits into, turning your joystick into a pistol or a rifle. I don’t have one of the controller frames yet but I want one. It’s so cool to be able to do drills to get better and then use those skills to smoke zombies. The first time I played, I ended up at it for hours. It’s realistic without being a one dimensional boring sport shooting game. I’m stoked to see what the new updates and improvements will be like when they come out. I think this is a game I will be following for a while. 

If you like shooting and you love killing zombies then I think you will want to try this out. It’s way more than just another zombie game. It brings a sense of realism missing from most shooters, and offers up the opportunity to learn and improve your skills in real time.

My wife was like “what are you doing? Running around our house shooting things?“ And I’m like “get to the Choppa!“ 

I love that it starts out in training mode because it makes me feel like I’m preparing for battle, and it gets you used to the game mechanics. But watching zombies try to breach my house and come around the corners of my actual kitchen hallway on to be met with two in the chest and one in the head never gets old. Y’all go play your toy games while I’m preparing for the apocalypse. We’ll see who last man standing is! LETS GOOOOOO! 🧟‍♂️🔫 💪

reviews.review_title: Is this what the future looks like?
reviews.review_description: You can place targets inside and outside of your house, while physically moving (no teleport) to engage through doors and windows. The zombies are the best looking and feeling of any MR game I've tried yet! Fully customizable stock support, and they have a pistol controller you can 3d print, that makes it super immersive!

After going through and figuring out the room setup, had a great time shooting zombies with a realistic looking gun! Got startled when I found one of the zombies right behind me in the nick of time lol. There's a lot of potential for fun running the drills too!
This is a great mixed reality game! I had a blast defending my play area from zombies and hitting targets throughout my apartment. Great work, and I can’t wait to see this game evolve!

Augmented Defender is a mixed reality shooting experience where you defend your real house from Zombies and Soldiers and can shoot challenging targets as well. This game innovates in so many ways. it has full support for custom gun controllers allowing you to use the best gun controllers available. I use my Ace VR handset. The developer of this app also is developing on the hardware side and has some amazing gun solutions available.  Multi modal support has just been introduced, allowing for the most realistic reloading experience in VR. The game is a heap of fun as well as being a great training tool for your shooting skills. The developer is available on discord and works great with the community on this game. This is clearly a passion project for the developer and it really shines through with the amount of awesome hard work put into it. If you like shooting guns and have a Quest, it's a must to try this out.

Seems like a good game/training tool if I could get it working the way I want. Trying to spread targets around my house is a mission in itself. Can't figure out how to raise targets off the ground, or adjust distance. For example I'm trying to have a target standing in  the door way of my bathroom and he's about 100 yards down the line and he's shrunk obv to simulate distance, can't figure out how to drag him closer. Or I try put one in my hall and he's sitting down. I would think there would be an option to move him higher. Even have him angled out a doorway..stuff like that...dunno if it's in the game and I just can't figure it out or it's missing. Tutorial video wouldn't play so I was guessing before I got fed up.
Make sure to get the accompanying hardware at the games website. It’s an amazing, low cost dry fire tool and really fun to use. The zombie mode got my heart pumping.

reviews.review_title: Great and useful,  one of the best
reviews.review_description: The app works great, with the handset it simulates better than any standard stock,  works great, aim is perfect, function is legit. Would recommend it to anyone looking for a fun game and simulated training.
